lines: You're okay. [long pause] You're safe. [long pause] I know it doesn't feel that way right now, but you are.

What's happening in your body is real. [long pause] Your heart is pounding. [long pause] Your chest is tight. [long pause] You might feel dizzy. [long pause] You might feel like you can't breathe. [long pause] That's your nervous system doing exactly what it was designed to do. [long pause] It's just doing it at the wrong time.
This will pass. [long pause] It always does. [long pause] And I'm going to help you get through it right now.

Here's what I need you to do. [long pause] We're going to use a technique called the physiological sigh. [long pause] It's the fastest way to calm your nervous system — it forces your lungs to offload carbon dioxide, which directly switches your body from panic mode to calm mode.
Here's how it works. [long pause] Two quick inhales through your nose, then one long, slow exhale through your mouth.
Short inhale through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] and a second, deeper inhale on top of that... [long pause] [long pause]
Now exhale slowly through your mouth. [long pause] Let it all go. [long pause] All of it.
Again. [long pause] Two inhales through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] short... [long pause] [long pause] then deep... [long pause] [long pause]
Long exhale through the mouth. [long pause] Slow it down. [long pause] Make the exhale last.
One more. [long pause] Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] deeper inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
And release. [long pause] Slow, steady exhale.
Good. [long pause] Your heart rate is already starting to come down. [long pause] You're doing this.
Now we're going to anchor you to what's real. [long pause] Right here. [long pause] Right now. [long pause] Not the story your mind is telling you — what's actually in front of you.
Look around the room. [long pause] Name five things you can see. [long pause] Anything. [long pause] A wall. [long pause] A light. [long pause] Your hands. [long pause] Just notice them.
Now four things you can hear. [long pause] Listen carefully. [long pause] Maybe it's your own breathing. [long pause] The hum of a room. [long pause] Anything.
Three things you can feel. [long pause] Your feet on the floor. [long pause] Fabric on your skin. [long pause] The weight of your hands.
One thing you can taste. [long pause] Even if it's just the inside of your mouth.
You're here. [long pause] You're present. [long pause] The wave is passing.
Be still and know that I am God.

Let that settle. [long pause] You don't have to fight this. [long pause] You don't have to fix anything right now. [long pause] The panic came. [long pause] And it's leaving. [long pause] That's what it does.
Take one more slow breath. [long pause] In through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] out through the mouth.
You made it through. [long pause] You're okay.
